title: Pole test
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"699fd","text":"This protocol describes the pole test for assessing general motor function in mice, including animal preparation, trial execution, and data analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7838l","text":"See the General considerations and habituation protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":8,"length":47}],"data":{}},{"key":"an3fe","text":"If experiment is done during the dark cycle, it is preferable to start the behaviour work at least one hour after the light-dark cycle change.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D020E10EDED84C3186848DE3BE27A4CB","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.kxygxj834l8j/v1"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"esar7","text":"For the experiment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4q79m","text":"- A cage filled with sawdust","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jsqr","text":"- A homemade 48 cm height metal rod of 1 cm diameter, covered with parafilm to facilitate traction, and with red adhesive tape to facilitate the placement of the mice. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pte8","text":"- A camera on a rack, connected to a computer. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5vnn9","text":"- The Capture Software (RRID:SCR_016047)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9iilt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhn63","text":"For the analysis:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b55ef","text":"- A media player as VLC","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Mice are acclimated in the room 01:00:00 before the experiment.
To assess their general motor functions, mice were tested on a pole test for 3 trials:
Repeat 3 times, each trial separated by 00:10:00 of resting.
Start recording with the camera using the Capture software (RRID:SCR_016047, version 2.4), 60 FPS.
Place the mouse on the pole: Hold the mouse on the surface of your hand and bring it up to the top of the pole. Position the mouse head-up at the top of the pole.
The mouse is acclimated to the cage for approximately 00:00:20 .
The analysis is done with a media video player:
Note the time required for the mouse to turn: from the moment the mouse is alone on the pole until the moment the mouse is completely turned around.
The time required for the mouse to descend to the floor level is recorded, with a maximum duration of 120s.
Once testing is completed, place the mouse back into its home cage and clean the metalrod before testing the next mouse.
Note the time necessary for the mouse to climb down completely: from the moment the mouse is completely turned around until the moment both front paws touch the ground.
creator: Amandine Even
